# VaneUI

> React component library powered by Tailwind CSS v4. Boolean-props API,
> mutually-exclusive prop categories, ThemeProvider for global defaults and
> overrides, native focus-visible outline on interactive components.

- Package: `@vaneui/ui`
- Repository: https://github.com/vaneui/vaneui
- Documentation: https://vaneui.com
- llms-full.txt: https://vaneui.com/llms-full.txt

## About

VaneUI is a React component library that uses a **boolean props API**: instead of
`<Button appearance="primary" size="lg" variant="filled">` you write
`<Button primary lg filled>`. Props are organized into mutually exclusive
categories (size, appearance, variant, shape, padding, etc.); only one value
per category is active at a time.

- Package: `@vaneui/ui`
- Install: `npm install @vaneui/ui`
- Peer deps: `react ^16.8` and `tailwindcss ^4`
- Status: 0.9.0 alpha (published under `@alpha` dist-tag; `@latest` is older)

Every component supports the same boolean prop categories where they apply.
Components default to sensible values (Button is `sm` + `primary` + `outline`;
Card is `md` + `primary` + `outline` + `rounded`; Typography defaults to
`inherit` appearance and `md` size; Row/Col/Stack default to `md` + `outline`)
so most JSX is brief.

Components that render an `<a>` when given `href` include: Button, IconButton,
Badge, Card, Chip, Code, Row, Col, Stack, Link, NavLink. All of these
auto-render a keyboard focus-visible outline in their interactive form
(always-on for NavLink, MenuItem, Link; conditional on `href` for the others;
opt out with `noFocusVisible`).

For complete documentation of every page in a single document, see
[`llms-full.txt`](https://vaneui.com/llms-full.txt).

## Docs

### Getting Started
- [Installation](https://vaneui.com/docs/getting-started/installation): Follow these steps to get the library installed and ready to use in your application.
- [Core Concepts](https://vaneui.com/docs/getting-started/core-concepts): Understand VaneUI's philosophy of boolean props, Tailwind CSS integration, and component customization.
- [Usage Basics](https://vaneui.com/docs/getting-started/usage-basics): Learn fundamental patterns and concepts for using VaneUI components effectively.

### Basic Components
- [Button](https://vaneui.com/docs/basic-components/button): Button triggers an action or event when clicked. It can contain text, an icon, or both, and is a primary way for users to interact with the app.
- [IconButton](https://vaneui.com/docs/basic-components/icon-button): A square icon-only button with customizable appearance, size, and shape. Supports loading state and renders as anchor when href is provided.
- [Input](https://vaneui.com/docs/basic-components/input): Input lets users enter text, numbers, and other data. A form element with support for various types, validation states, and styling options.
- [Checkbox](https://vaneui.com/docs/basic-components/checkbox): Allows users to select one or more options from a set. Checkboxes are ideal for binary choices and multiple selections within forms.
- [Label](https://vaneui.com/docs/basic-components/label): Label displays text for categorization, status indicators, or highlighting information. Can be styled with different colors, sizes, and variants.
- [Badge](https://vaneui.com/docs/basic-components/badge): Badge highlights information such as notifications or counts in a non-intrusive way. Often paired with other elements like icons or navigation links.
- [Chip](https://vaneui.com/docs/basic-components/chip): Compact token for tags, attributes, or other discrete entities inline with text. Defaults to secondary appearance, outline variant, and rounded shape.
- [Code](https://vaneui.com/docs/basic-components/code): Renders inline code snippets in a themed monospace surface. Use for commands, file paths, or technical terms in prose; renders as an anchor when given href.
- [Kbd](https://vaneui.com/docs/basic-components/kbd): Displays keyboard keys and shortcuts with monospace font, border, and a raised 3D effect. Ideal for documenting keyboard shortcuts.
- [Mark](https://vaneui.com/docs/basic-components/mark): Highlights text with a background color for emphasis. Defaults to warning (yellow) appearance for a natural highlighter effect.
- [Icon](https://vaneui.com/docs/basic-components/icon): A lightweight SVG wrapper that provides consistent sizing, color inheritance, and themed appearances for icons.

### Layout Components
- [Container](https://vaneui.com/docs/layout-components/container): Manages the main content area by centering it and applying a max-width. It ensures a consistent and readable layout across different screen sizes.
- [Section](https://vaneui.com/docs/layout-components/section): Groups related content and owns the outer padding and background of a page band. Renders as a plain wrapper by default; pass tag="section" for a landmark.
- [Row](https://vaneui.com/docs/layout-components/row): A fundamental layout component that arranges its children in a horizontal line. It is built on the flexbox model and is used to create columns.
- [Col](https://vaneui.com/docs/layout-components/col): Col organizes content vertically within a Row, creating responsive column-based layouts.
- [Stack](https://vaneui.com/docs/layout-components/stack): Stack arranges its children with consistent spacing. Works for both vertical and horizontal layouts.
- [Card](https://vaneui.com/docs/layout-components/card): Card is a container that groups related content with consistent styling. It can contain text, images, and other components.
- [Grid2](https://vaneui.com/docs/layout-components/grid2): A responsive layout component for creating a two-column grid. Ideal for side-by-side content layouts and balanced arrangements.
- [Grid3](https://vaneui.com/docs/layout-components/grid3): A specialized layout component for creating a responsive three-column grid. It simplifies the arrangement of content into a balanced and organized structure.
- [Grid4](https://vaneui.com/docs/layout-components/grid4): A responsive layout component that arranges its children into a four-column grid. This is ideal for displaying a collection of items or features.
- [Grid5](https://vaneui.com/docs/layout-components/grid5): A responsive layout component that creates a five-column grid. Perfect for organizing content into balanced, visually appealing arrangements.
- [Grid6](https://vaneui.com/docs/layout-components/grid6): A six-column grid layout component for creating compact, organized displays. Ideal for icon grids, feature collections, and detailed content arrangements.
- [Divider](https://vaneui.com/docs/layout-components/divider): Renders a thin line to separate content and create a clear visual hierarchy. Dividers can be used to group related items in lists and layouts.
- [Img](https://vaneui.com/docs/layout-components/img): Displays images with VaneUI styling support including sizes, shapes, borders, shadows, and object-fit options.
- [Table](https://vaneui.com/docs/layout-components/table): Renders semantic table markup with Table, Thead, Tbody, Tfoot, Tr, Th, Td, and Caption. The size prop cascades from the table to every cell, and a cell or row can override it.

### Overlay Components
- [Overlay](https://vaneui.com/docs/overlay-components/overlay): A fullscreen backdrop overlay for creating modal backgrounds, loading screens, and lightbox effects. Renders via portal with click-to-close and optional blur.
- [Modal](https://vaneui.com/docs/overlay-components/modal): Modal is an accessible dialog with focus trapping, scroll lock, and keyboard navigation. Renders its portal backdrop with role="dialog" and aria-modal="true".
- [Popup](https://vaneui.com/docs/overlay-components/popup): A floating element anchored to a trigger element using CSS Anchor Positioning. Supports 12 placement options, width matching, and click-outside dismissal.
- [Menu](https://vaneui.com/docs/overlay-components/menu): A dropdown menu triggered by a button with full keyboard navigation. Contains MenuItem, Divider, and MenuLabel subcomponents.

### Navigation Components
- [NavLink](https://vaneui.com/docs/navigation-components/navlink): A navigation link for sidebars, nav menus, and headers. Supports active state, icons, and renders as anchor or button.

### Typography Components
- [Text](https://vaneui.com/docs/typography-components/text): The primary component for rendering all text content. It provides props to control typographic properties like size, weight, color, and alignment.
- [PageTitle](https://vaneui.com/docs/typography-components/page-title): A specialized component for the primary heading of a page. It ensures consistent styling for top-level titles across your application.
- [SectionTitle](https://vaneui.com/docs/typography-components/section-title): A component for rendering headings of major sections within a page. It helps to create a clear and accessible document structure.
- [Title](https://vaneui.com/docs/typography-components/title): Renders a heading to establish a semantic hierarchy on the page. Use this component for the main titles of pages or sections.
- [Link](https://vaneui.com/docs/typography-components/link): Renders an accessible and themeable anchor link for navigation. Use this to direct users to other pages or external websites.
- [List](https://vaneui.com/docs/typography-components/list): A container for displaying a series of related items in an ordered or unordered fashion. It provides consistent styling for list elements.
- [ListItem](https://vaneui.com/docs/typography-components/list-item): A single entry inside a List. Renders an <li> with Text's typography props (minus margin, letter-spacing, cursor), plus an icon prop for the marker.
- [Blockquote](https://vaneui.com/docs/typography-components/blockquote): Displays quoted content with a left border accent. Inherits appearance from the parent by default, so it blends into themed containers.

### Customization
- [Theming Overview](https://vaneui.com/docs/customization/theming-overview): Understand VaneUI's theming system and design token architecture.
- [Using ThemeProvider](https://vaneui.com/docs/customization/using-theme-provider): Configure and customize themes throughout your application with ThemeProvider.
- [Theme & ThemeOverride](https://vaneui.com/docs/customization/theme-and-override): Customize themes using ThemeProvider's theme and themeOverride properties (applied to providers, not components).
- [ThemeDefaults](https://vaneui.com/docs/customization/theme-defaults): Set default theme values across your application using themeDefaults.
- [ExtraClasses](https://vaneui.com/docs/customization/extra-classes): Apply additional CSS classes to components using extraClasses.
- [CSS Variables](https://vaneui.com/docs/customization/css-variables): Customize VaneUI components using CSS custom properties and variables.
- [Customizing Styles](https://vaneui.com/docs/customization/customizing-styles): Override VaneUI component styles using Tailwind CSS classes, CSS variables, and the className prop.
- [Variant Inheritance](https://vaneui.com/docs/customization/variant-inheritance): How components inherit colors from ancestor layouts via CSS custom-property cascade.

### Reference
- [Common Props](https://vaneui.com/docs/reference/common-props): Layout and typography props shared across all components.
